themes.topic: Kimi K3's Performance, Economic Impact, and Implications for AI Model Competition
themes.summary: The release of Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion parameter open-source model, has sparked debate on AI model economics; while its token inefficiency makes it expensive per task, it demonstrates China's frontier compute capability, validates scaling laws, and intensifies competition, potentially benefiting AI infrastructure by lowering model layer margins.

mentions.text:
Did Kimi just start another DeepSeek moment?

Kimi released the largest open weight model ever yesterday. It scored 57 on the Artificial Analysis index, 3 points behind Claude Fable 5, causing AI stocks to panic.

The bears say intelligence is becoming a commodity. Open models fr

Kimi K3 may be an important inflection point for AI. Potentially negative for Anthropic and OpenAI while being net positive for essentially every other company in the world. I mean that very literally. Although the real “Sputnik moment” would be an open-source frontier model that

k3 is v good.

it’s also NOT cheap (stop calling it step-function cheaper), was trained on the most advanced NVDA gpus and benefited from distillation.

this is not evidence that compute stopped mattering. it’s evidence that china has enough compute to build near the frontier.

Research|LLM: Kimi K3 - Scaling Still Works; An Expensive Model Competing at Front Tier

Kimi K3’s overall performance now sits in the global top tier. It is a large 2.8-trillion-parameter MoE model with a 1-million-token context window, and it stands out in tests spanning coding

🤣🤣🤣🤣🤣
This is why open source is so important
Kimi mogged Opus so hard
Ant was forced to keep Fable in the subscription

Fable vs Kimi K3 in Tetris

Fable wins https://t.co/Trmjch4HMJ

themes.topic: Market Correction Driven by Deleveraging & Liquidation Cascades
themes.summary: The market is experiencing a significant correction, particularly in beta/momentum stocks, driven by unprecedented leverage, widespread margin calls, and forced liquidations in the Korean market and broader AI-related assets, leading some to anticipate generational buying opportunities.

mentions.text:

“As of July 13, over 1.2 million leveraged retail traders' accounts hit margin call lines, with 320,000 to 360,000 accounts force-liquidated by brokers.”

(There were other sources clai

Market could get worse for the next few weeks.

There are unprecedented amounts of leverage (not just Korea) out there right now causing a domino effect of margin calls and liquidations. 

I continue to hold my memory positions, underlying thesis is unchanged.

Prepare for the wo
Exited $NBIS last night when Asia started dying again. Haven't bought back yet. Want to give the margin calls and forced selling in AI a little more time to burn out.

themes.topic: AI Datacenter Optical Interconnect Layers & InP Light Source
themes.summary: PhotonCap details the three layers of AI datacenter optical interconnect (in-rack, intra-datacenter, inter-datacenter) and highlights the common InP light source across all, noting META's Prometheus project extends inter-datacenter links up to 2,000 km.

mentions.text:
Map AI infrastructure by distance and the optics split into three layers.

- 1m to 100m, in-rack: NVLink, copper, short-reach CPO (scale-up)

- 1km, intra-datacenter: PAM4 direct-detect transceivers (scale-out)

- 1km to 3,000km, inter-datacenter: DWDM plus coherent ZR/ZR+ (scale

themes.topic: Ciena's Role in AI Datacenter Interconnect & Hyper-Rail
themes.summary: Crux Capital details Ciena's strong position in connecting AI data centers across vast distances, driven by demand for coherent optics and DCI, with Hyper-Rail poised as a major future growth driver. Ciena has secured a hyperscaler order for Hyper-Rail, with ramp starting in 2027.

themes.topic: Goldman Sachs Raises Innolight PT on Strong Optical Demand
themes.summary: Goldman Sachs significantly raised Innolight's price target and future earnings estimates, citing much higher silicon photonics module volumes, increased AI capex, and strong demand for 1.6T/3.2T modules, indicating robust growth in the optical sector.

themes.topic: VIAVI's Role in Optical Buildout Testing & Increased Test Intensity
themes.summary: Crux Capital highlights VIAVI's critical role in testing the optical buildout, covering various datacenter components from silicon photonics to physical fiber networks, noting that increasing complexity in technologies like CPO and advanced packaging drives higher test intensity and demand.

themes.topic: Memory Stock Decline Driven by Deleveraging and Future Supply Expansion
themes.summary: Jukan05 presents an analysis suggesting the decline in memory stocks is due to unwinding leveraged positions and the market pricing in future supply expansion from Samsung and SK Hynix, expected to ease supply-demand imbalance by 2028.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: SK Hynix ADR-Local Share Convertibility & Arbitrage
themes.summary: Aleabitoreddit notes that SK Hynix ADRs and local shares become convertible on July 29, which is expected to open arbitrage opportunities and likely compress the current 25%+ ADR premium.

themes.topic: Meta in Talks to Lease Compute to Anthropic ($10B Deal)
themes.summary: Meta is reportedly in talks to lease compute capacity to Anthropic in a $10 billion deal, which further validates the business models of neocloud providers.

themes.summary: TSMC reported strong Q2 2026 earnings, beating revenue, gross margin, operating margin, net profit, and EPS guidance, with revenue up 33.7% YoY.
